title: Nouvelles options de transformation d’image
code: # Augmenter le contraste (>1) ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, contrast = 1.5) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")  # Diminuer le contraste (<1)   ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, contrast = 0.7) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")

title: Nouvelles options de transformation d’image
code: # Gamma < 1 = tons moyens plus clairs ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, gamma = 0.8) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")  # Gamma > 1 = tons moyens plus foncés ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, gamma = 1.2) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")

title: Nouvelles options de transformation d’image
code: # Saturation 0 = noir et blanc ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, saturation = 0) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")  # Saturation 0.5 = désaturé à 50% ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, saturation = 0.5) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")  # Saturation 2 = très saturé ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, saturation = 2) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")

title: Nouvelles options de transformation d’image
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,                grayscale = FALSE,               saturation = 0.7,    # Légèrement désaturé               brightness = 1.1,    # Un peu plus lumineux               contrast = 1.3,      # Plus de contraste               gamma = 0.9) +       # Tons moyens éclaircis   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "white")

title: Nouvelles options de transformation d’image
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               saturation = 0.3,    # Très désaturé               brightness = 0.9,    # Légèrement plus sombre               contrast = 1.2,      # Plus de contraste               gamma = 1.1) +       # Tons moyens assombris   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "brown")

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: ggbasemap R package makes easy add basemap tiles XYZ tile servers (like OpenStreetMap, Google, ESRI) ggplot2 plots. automatically detects bounding boxes, calculates appropriate zoom levels, provides powerful image styling options.

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: Automatic zoom calculation: need guess right zoom level Multiple tile sources: Works OpenStreetMap, Google, ESRI, CartoDB, Image transformations: Grayscale, saturation, brightness, contrast, gamma Map rotation: Rotate maps angle Oblique Mercator projection Web Mercator native: Tiles kept EPSG:3857 sharpest display sf compatible: Works seamlessly sf objects

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: simplest way use ggbasemap sf object: Basic map OpenStreetMap basemap : 1. Extract bounding box nc 2. Calculate optimal zoom level 3. Download tiles OpenStreetMap 4. Display background layer
code: # Load required libraries library(ggbasemap) library(ggplot2) library(sf)  # Read spatial data (included with sf package) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)  # Create map with basemap ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red", linewidth = 1)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: data data frame longitude latitude columns:
code: df <- data.frame(   lon = c(-74.0060, -73.9352),   lat = c(40.7128, 40.7306),   city = c("NYC", "Manhattan") )  ggplot() +   add_basemap(df, "lon", "lat") +   geom_point(data = df, aes(x = lon, y = lat), color = "red", size = 3)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: can easily switch different tile providers:
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: data: sf object data frame containing spatial data x, y: Column names longitude latitude (data frames) bbox: Optional bounding box override automatic detection url: Tile server URL template zoom: Zoom level (auto-calculated provided)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: alpha: Transparency (0 = invisible, 1 = opaque) interpolate: Whether interpolate pixels (default: TRUE) padding: Extra space around data (default: 0.1 = 10%)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: grayscale: Convert black white (TRUE/FALSE) saturation: Color saturation (0 = grayscale, 1 = normal, >1 = vivid) brightness: Brightness multiplier (1 = normal, <1 = darker, >1 = brighter) contrast: Contrast adjustment (1 = normal, <1 = flat, >1 = punchy) gamma: Gamma correction (1 = normal, <1 = brighter midtones, >1 = darker midtones)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc) +   geom_sf(data = nc)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, alpha = 0.7, zoom = 15) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, grayscale = TRUE, brightness = 0.9, contrast = 1.2) +   geom_sf(data = nc, color = "white")

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: package auto-calculates zoom level, sometimes want control: Low zoom (5-8): Regional/country views Medium zoom (9-12): City/state views High zoom (13-16): Neighborhood/street views high (17-19): Building-level detail

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: large geographic areas, consider:
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, zoom = 6, padding = 0.05) +   geom_sf(data = nc)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
code: ggplot() +   # Base layer: basemap   add_basemap(nc, alpha = 0.6) +   # Middle layer: boundaries   geom_sf(data = nc, aes(fill = AREA), alpha = 0.5) +   # Legend and theme   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   theme_minimal()

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: get “fetch tiles” errors: Check internet connection Try different tile URL corporate networks block tile servers Use verbose = TRUE see ’s happening:
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, verbose = TRUE) +   geom_sf(data = nc)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: auto-zoom adds +1 sharper images, can increase :
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, zoom = 16) +   geom_sf(data = nc)

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: Learn Map Rotation create angled maps Explore Image Transformations styled basemaps Check FAQ common questions

title: Introduction to ggbasemap
text: ggplot2 documentation sf package documentation ggspatial documentation Slippy Map Tilenames - Technical details XYZ tiles

title: Map Rotation
text: Map rotation allows present spatial data angle, can useful : Fitting rectangular maps better page layouts Emphasizing certain geographic features Creating visually interesting presentations Aligning natural geographic orientations (e.g., coastlines, mountain ranges) ggbasemap uses Oblique Mercator projection achieve smooth rotation maintaining accurate geographic relationships.

title: Map Rotation
text: rotate map, use coord_rotate() function: Map rotated 45 degrees coord_rotate() function returns list : coord: coord_sf object applies rotation bbox: expanded bounding box covers rotated area crs: coordinate reference system string used angle: rotation angle degrees
code: # Get rotation parameters for 45 degrees rot <- coord_rotate(nc, angle = 45) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on  # Create the rotated map ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, bbox = rot$bbox) +   geom_sf(data = nc) +   rot$coord +   ggtitle("North Carolina rotated 45°")

title: Map Rotation
text: Different rotation angles produce different visual effects: Comparison rotation angles
code: # Create a 2-panel comparison library(patchwork)  # No rotation rot0 <- coord_rotate(nc, 0) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on p0 <- ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, bbox = rot0$bbox) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red") +   rot0$coord +   ggtitle("0° (No Rotation)") +   theme_void()  # 45 degree rotation rot45 <- coord_rotate(nc, 45) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on p45 <- ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, bbox = rot45$bbox) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "blue") +   rot45$coord +   ggtitle("45° Rotation") +   theme_void()  # Display side by side p0 + p45

title: Map Rotation
text: Rotation works perfectly image transformations: Rotated styled basemap
code: rot <- coord_rotate(nc, 45) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on  ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,                bbox = rot$bbox,               grayscale = TRUE,               contrast = 1.2) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red") +   rot$coord +   ggtitle("Rotated + Styled")

title: Map Rotation
text: can control aspect ratio (width/height) rotated map using ratio parameter: Different aspect ratios Common ratio values: - 16/9 widescreen displays presentations - 4/3 standard presentation format - 1 square format (social media, posters) - Custom values like 2 0.5 specific layouts
code: # Create comparison of different ratios rot_169 <- coord_rotate(nc, 45, ratio = 16/9) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on rot_11 <- coord_rotate(nc, 45, ratio = 1) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on  p169 <- ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, bbox = rot_169$bbox) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "darkblue") +   rot_169$coord +   ggtitle("16:9 Widescreen") +   theme_void()  p11 <- ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, bbox = rot_11$bbox) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "darkgreen") +   rot_11$coord +   ggtitle("1:1 Square") +   theme_void()  p169 + p11

title: Map Rotation
text: ’s complete example combining multiple features: Complete example rotation
code: library(viridis) #> Loading required package: viridisLite  # Calculate rotation rot <- coord_rotate(nc, 30) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on  # Create the map ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,                bbox = rot$bbox,               saturation = 0.4,               brightness = 1.05) +   geom_sf(data = nc,            aes(fill = AREA),           color = "white",           linewidth = 0.3,           alpha = 0.8) +   rot$coord +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma") +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   labs(title = "North Carolina Counties",        subtitle = "Rotated 30° for optimal layout")

title: Map Rotation
text: Read Image Transformations style basemaps Check FAQ common questions

title: Image Transformations
text: ggbasemap provides powerful image transformation capabilities allow style basemaps match visualization needs. can adjust grayscale, saturation, brightness, contrast, gamma correction.

title: Image Transformations
text: Convert basemap black white classic look: Grayscale basemap perfect : - Academic publications - Print media - want data stand - Creating vintage-style maps
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, grayscale = TRUE) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 1) +   theme_void()

title: Image Transformations
text: Control intensity colors: Normal saturation Reduced saturation
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, saturation = 1) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red") +   ggtitle("Saturation = 1 (default)") ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, saturation = 0.5) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red") +   ggtitle("Saturation = 0.5")

title: Image Transformations
text: Adjust overall lightness: Adjusted brightness
code: # Darker (-30%) ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, brightness = 0.7) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "yellow") +   ggtitle("Brightness = 0.7 (darker)")

title: Image Transformations
text: Enhance reduce difference light dark areas: High contrast
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, contrast = 1.5) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red") +   ggtitle("Contrast = 1.5 (punchy)")

title: Image Transformations
text: Adjust midtones without affecting blacks whites: Gamma correction
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, gamma = 0.8) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red") +   ggtitle("Gamma = 0.8 (brighter midtones)")

title: Image Transformations
text: real power comes combining multiple transformations:

title: Image Transformations
text: Vintage style map
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               saturation = 0.3,               brightness = 0.85,               contrast = 1.2,               gamma = 1.1) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "brown", linewidth = 0.8) +   theme_void() +   ggtitle("Vintage Style")

title: Image Transformations
text: High contrast monochrome
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               grayscale = TRUE,               brightness = 1.1,               contrast = 1.6) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "black") +   theme_void() +   ggtitle("High-Contrast Monochrome")

title: Image Transformations
text: Soft faded look
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               saturation = 0.6,               brightness = 1.1,               contrast = 0.8,               gamma = 0.9) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "darkblue") +   theme_void() +   ggtitle("Soft/Faded Look")

title: Image Transformations
text: want data star: Data-friendly background
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               saturation = 0.2,               brightness = 1.05,               contrast = 0.9) +   geom_sf(data = nc,            aes(fill = AREA),           color = "white",           linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   theme_void() +   ggtitle("Data-Friendly Background")

title: Image Transformations
text: Transformations applied order: Gamma (non-linear correction) Brightness (linear multiplication) Contrast (centered 0.5) Saturation (color adjustment) Grayscale (final conversion TRUE) order ensures best visual results.

title: Image Transformations
text: Print-ready grayscale
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               grayscale = TRUE,               contrast = 1.2,               brightness = 0.95) +   geom_sf(data = nc)

title: Image Transformations
text: Web-optimized colors
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               saturation = 1.1,               brightness = 1.05,               contrast = 1.0) +   geom_sf(data = nc)

title: Image Transformations
text: Don’t overdo : Extreme values (>2 <0.3) usually look bad Check data: Make sure data colors work basemap Test different screens: looks good monitor might others

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
text: Add XYZ tile basemaps ggplot2 automatic zoom calculation, image transformations, map rotation.

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
text: ggbasemap makes effortless add basemap tiles services like OpenStreetMap, Google, ESRI, CartoDB ggplot2 visualizations. automatically detects data extents, calculates optimal zoom levels, provides powerful image styling options.

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
text: 🗺️ Automatic Everything: Detects bounding boxes calculates optimal zoom 🎨 Image Styling: Grayscale, saturation, brightness, contrast, gamma adjustment 🔄 Map Rotation: Rotate maps angle Oblique Mercator projection 🚀 High Performance: Native Web Mercator (EPSG:3857) sharpest display 🔧 Flexible: Works sf objects, data frames, manual bounding boxes 📱 Multiple Sources: OpenStreetMap, Google, ESRI, CartoDB, custom XYZ tiles

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: # Install from CRAN (when available) install.packages("ggbasemap")  # Or the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("yourusername/ggbasemap")

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: library(ggbasemap) library(ggplot2) library(sf)  # Read spatial data nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  # Create map with basemap ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red", linewidth = 1)

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: df <- data.frame(   lon = c(-74.0060, -73.9352),   lat = c(40.7128, 40.7306),   city = c("NYC", "Manhattan") )  ggplot() +   add_basemap(df, "lon", "lat") +   geom_point(data = df, aes(x = lon, y = lat), color = "red", size = 3)

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: # Grayscale ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, grayscale = TRUE) +   geom_sf(data = nc)  # Styled - Vintage look ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,                saturation = 0.3,               brightness = 0.9,               contrast = 1.2) +   geom_sf(data = nc)  # High contrast satellite ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               url = "https://mt1.google.com/vt/lyrs=y&x={x}&y={y}&z={z}",               contrast = 1.4,               gamma = 0.9) +   geom_sf(data = nc)

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: # Rotate map 45 degrees rot <- coord_rotate(nc, 45)  ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, bbox = rot$bbox) +   geom_sf(data = nc) +   rot$coord

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
text: Full documentation available https://cedricbouffard.github.io/ggbasemap Introduction: Get started ggbasemap Image Transformations: Style basemaps Map Rotation: Create rotated maps

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
text: OpenStreetMap (default): https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png Google Satellite: https://mt1.google.com/vt/lyrs=y&x={x}&y={y}&z={z} ESRI World Imagery: https://server.arcgisonline.com/ArcGIS/rest/services/World_Imagery/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x} CartoDB Positron: https://.basemaps.cartocdn.com/light_all/{z}/{x}/{y}.png CartoDB Dark Matter: https://.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}.png OpenTopoMap: https://.tile.opentopomap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
text: add_basemap(): Add basemap tiles ggplot2 coord_rotate(): Create rotated coordinate systems

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: # Clean, minimal ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, grayscale = TRUE, contrast = 1.2) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "black") +   theme_void()  # Data-focused ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, saturation = 0.2) +   geom_sf(data = nc, aes(fill = value)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   theme_minimal()  # High-quality satellite ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc,               url = "ESRI_URL",               zoom = 14,               contrast = 1.3,               gamma = 0.9) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "yellow")

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(data,                grayscale = TRUE,  # Print-friendly               contrast = 1.2) +  # Better ink coverage   geom_sf(data = data) +   theme_void()

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(data,               saturation = 1.1,  # More vibrant               brightness = 1.05) + # Brighter for screens   geom_sf(data = data)

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2
code: ggplot() +   add_basemap(big_country,                zoom = 6,        # Lower zoom               padding = 0.05) + # Less padding   geom_sf(data = big_country)

what: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2 — add_basemap
title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2 — add_basemap
text: Adds basemap tiles XYZ tile server ggplot2 plot. Uses ggspatial proper coordinate management tile stitching. Automatically calculates appropriate zoom level.

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2 — add_basemap
code: add_basemap(   data = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   bbox = NULL,   crs = 4326,   url = "https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png",   zoom = NULL,   alpha = 1,   zoom_offset = 1,   interpolate = TRUE,   padding = 0.1,   grayscale = FALSE,   saturation = 1,   brightness = 1,   contrast = 1,   gamma = 1,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2 — add_basemap
text: data data frame sf object containing spatial data (optional bbox provided) x name longitude column. Can unquoted (bare name) quoted (character string). Optional data sf object bbox provided. y name latitude column. Can unquoted (bare name) quoted (character string). Optional data sf object bbox provided. bbox Bounding box c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) sf bbox object WGS84 (EPSG:4326). Optional data provided. crs Coordinate reference system (default: 4326 WGS84). Can EPSG code crs object. url XYZ tile URL template x, y, z placeholders zoom Zoom level (optional, auto-calculated provided) alpha Transparency basemap (0-1) zoom_offset Number zoom levels add auto-calculated zoom (default 1). Use 0 auto zoom without offset, 2 higher resolution. interpolate Whether interpolate raster image padding Fraction range add padding around data (default 0.1) grayscale Convert image grayscale (default FALSE) saturation Saturation multiplier (1 = change, 0 = grayscale, >1 = saturated, default 1) brightness Brightness multiplier (1 = change, <1 = darker, >1 = brighter, default 1) contrast Contrast multiplier (1 = change, <1 = less contrast, >1 = contrast, default 1) gamma Gamma correction value (1 = change, <1 = brighter midtones, >1 = darker midtones, default 1) verbose Print debugging information (default FALSE)

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2 — add_basemap
text: ggplot2 layer (list layers) can added plot

title: Add Basemap Tiles to ggplot2 — add_basemap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  # Method 1: Using a data frame with bare column names df <- data.frame(lon = c(-74.0, -73.9), lat = c(40.7, 40.8)) ggplot() +   add_basemap(df, lon, lat) +   geom_point(data = df, aes(x = lon, y = lat), color = "red", size = 3)  # Method 1b: Using a data frame with quoted column names ggplot() +   add_basemap(df, "lon", "lat") +   geom_point(data = df, aes(x = lon, y = lat), color = "red", size = 3)  # Method 2: Using an sf object library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package="sf")) ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc) +   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red")  # Method 3: Using ggspatial layer_spatial ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc) +   layer_spatial(nc, fill = NA, color = "red")  # Method 4: Using bbox directly bbox <- c(xmin = -74.1, ymin = 40.6, xmax = -73.8, ymax = 40.9) ggplot() +   add_basemap(bbox = bbox) +   geom_point(data = df, aes(x = lon, y = lat), color = "red", size = 3)  # Method 5: Adjust zoom level for sharper images ggplot() +   add_basemap(nc, zoom_offset = 2) +  # Higher resolution   geom_sf(data = nc, fill = NA, color = "red") } # }

what: Create Coordinate System with Rotation — coord_rotate
title: Create Coordinate System with Rotation — coord_rotate
text: Creates coord_sf rotation applied using Oblique Mercator projection. Also returns expanded bbox needed cover rotated area.

title: Create Coordinate System with Rotation — coord_rotate
code: coord_rotate(x, angle, ratio = NULL)

title: Create Coordinate System with Rotation — coord_rotate
text: x sf object base extent angle Rotation angle degrees ratio Aspect ratio (width/height) output bbox. Default NULL uses natural ratio. Common values: 16/9 widescreen, 4/3 standard, 1 square.

title: Create Coordinate System with Rotation — coord_rotate
text: List :   - `coord`: coord_sf object rotated view   - `bbox`: Expanded bbox WGS84 covering rotated area (adjusted ratio specified)   - `crs`: CRS string used rotation   - `angle`: Rotation angle degrees   - `ratio`: Aspect ratio (specified)

title: Rotation Matrix — rot
text: Creates 2x2 rotation matrix given angle

title: Rotation Matrix — rot
code: rot(a)

title: Rotation Matrix — rot
text: Angle degrees

title: Rotation Matrix — rot
text: 2x2 rotation matrix

text: Added grayscale parameter convert basemaps black & white Added saturation parameter control color intensity Added brightness parameter adjust overall lightness Added contrast parameter contrast adjustment Added gamma parameter gamma correction Transformations can combined complex effects

title: ggbasemap 0.2.0 (Development)
text: Added coord_rotate() function rotating maps Uses Oblique Mercator projection accurate rotation Automatically calculates expanded bounding box Supports rotation angle (positive negative)

title: ggbasemap 0.2.0 (Development)
text: Tiles now stored Web Mercator (EPSG:3857) sharpest display Auto-calculated zoom level now +1 sharper images Better error messages verbose mode Improved tile fetching retry logic

title: ggbasemap 0.2.0 (Development)
text: Fixed saturation transformation working correctly Fixed handling named bbox vectors Fixed S2 geometry engine compatibility issues Fixed CRS string format older PROJ versions
